summary: IDF conducted targeted strikes in Gaza eliminating senior Hamas commanders, including the head of the Khan Younis tunnel unit and the commander of the Central Camps naval police. An IDF officer, Sgt. David Hazutt, was killed in a Hezbollah engagement in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon, during which the attacking militant was subsequently eliminated. The US military launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a commercial oil tanker, while Iran reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain.
key_points: IDF eliminated Kamal Mohamed Hamdan Nagar, head of the tunnel unit in Khan Younis, and Abd al-Rahman Maher Abd al-Karim Ziada, a Nukhba squad commander who infiltrated Israel during the October 7 massacre, in separate strikes last week. (Source: @New_security8200/202127)

IDF airstrike in central Gaza killed Mansour Sami Mahmoud Shihata, commander of the Hamas naval police in the Central Camps, along with two other naval police commanders who were advancing terror plots against IDF forces. (Source: @New_security8200/202129)

Sgt. David Hazutt, 21, a platoon commander in Battalion 12, Golani Brigade, was killed in a Hezbollah militant encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; the militant was later located and eliminated by IDF forces. (Source: @New_security8200/202132, @New_security8200/202136)

US military launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz early Sunday following a Saturday morning attack on a commercial oil tanker in the waterway. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715)

Explosions were reported in the Sirik and Bandar Abbas areas of Iran, with projectiles striking a communications tower in the Tahroviye village area of Sirik and a naval base, while additional explosions were reported near Qeshm and Bandar Lengeh. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715, @VahidOnline/76721)
Iran reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, according to unconfirmed security reports. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93285, @lelotsenzura/93283)

Iraqi security forces conducted a sweeping wave of arrests in Baghdad overnight, including sitting politicians and government figures, with 47 officials arrested on corruption charges; Iraqi intelligence also reported large security deployments including tanks in Baghdad's Green Zone. (Source: @asafroz/50811, @asafroz/50814, @shin_persian/7266, @naya_foriraq/80218)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir approved plans for continued operations in the Northern Command, stating the agreement with Lebanon's government is historic and that Israel is prepared to return to rapid warfare in Lebanon and Iran if required. (Source: @idf_telegram/24464, @inon_yttach/9195)

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is the US military launching retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a commercial oil tanker, marking a direct escalation between US and Iranian forces in a critical maritime chokepoint. Concurrently, IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon resulted in the death of an IDF officer during a Hezbollah engagement, while targeted airstrikes in Gaza eliminated multiple senior Hamas commanders. Iran also reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, broadening the regional confrontation.

points: The US military launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz early Sunday, 7 Tir (June 28, 2026), in response to a Saturday morning attack on a commercial oil tanker in the waterway, as confirmed by a US official speaking to Axios. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715)

Explosions were reported in the Sirik area of Iran, with projectiles striking a communications tower in the Tahroviye village area of Sirik; a naval base in Tahroviye Sirik was also hit by missiles, with the impact felt on the ground. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715)

Additional explosions were reported in Bandar Abbas, with at least two missiles launched toward the sea at approximately 03:36-03:39 local time, and further explosions reported near Qeshm toward Sooza around 00:45 and in Hormozgan province. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715, @VahidOnline/76721)

Passage through the Strait of Hormuz still requires coordination with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards, according to IRIB, and maritime traffic data shows only approximately 50 oil tankers and 40 containers transited in June 2026 compared to 1,150 tankers and 400 containers in December 2025. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4466, @SharghDaily/168642)
Iran reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain according to unconfirmed security reports from the field. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93285, @lelotsenzura/93283)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Iraq's new PM al-Zaidi in Baghdad as part of an official visit to Iraq. (Source: @presstv/196030)
Talks scheduled for this week in Switzerland between Tehran and Washington were cancelled following the renewal of fighting, according to the Wall Street Journal. (Source: @yediotnews25/132027)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir approved operational plans in the Northern Command, stating the agreement signed with Lebanon's government is historic and significant, and that Israel is prepared to return to rapid warfare in both Lebanon and Iran if required. (Source: @idf_telegram/24464, @inon_yttach/9195)

Sgt. David Hazutt, 21, from Ashkelon, a platoon commander in Battalion 12, Golani Brigade, was killed during a Hezbollah militant encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; another soldier sustained light injuries and the militant was subsequently located and eliminated in a nearby building. (Source: @New_security8200/202132, @New_security8200/202136)

The IDF is set to demolish a large Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, with residents of the Upper Galilee warned to expect a high-intensity explosion in the coming hours that may. (Source: @manniefabian/58699)

Reports indicate a heated clash between Prime Minister Netanyahu and Ambassador to Washington Yechiel Leiter over a proposed Lebanon deal, with Leiter insisting the agreement represents a crucial achievement Israel should sign immediately. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4481)

Israeli intelligence officials warned the US that Hamas's military wing is regrouping for renewed war, producing hundreds of IEDs and anti-tank missiles monthly, recruiting fighters aged 18-22, conducting elite unit training, and attempting to smuggle drones and communications equipment from Sinai; the US opposes returning to combat. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80249)

Details of the Lebanon-Israel security framework agreement indicate it has shifted from a fixed timeline to field-based assessment, with a pilot project for Lebanese army entry into specific areas to disarm Hezbollah set to begin within coming weeks; Beirut requested Washington not publish a confidential security annex. (Source: @SharghDaily/168636)

IDF airstrikes in the Shati area last week eliminated Abd al-Rahman Maher Abd al-Karim Ziada, a Nukhba squad commander in Hamas's military wing who infiltrated Israeli territory during the October 7 massacre and looted IDF vehicles into Gaza. (Source: @New_security8200/202127)

In a separate strike in the Khan Younis area on the same day, the IDF eliminated Kamal Mohamed Hamdan Nagar, head of the tunnel unit in Khan Younis within Hamas's military wing. (Source: @New_security8200/202127)

IDF airstrike in central Gaza, directed by Shin Bet, killed Mansour Sami Mahmoud Shihata, commander of the Hamas naval police in the Central Camps, along with two other naval police commanders; all three were armed and advancing terror plots against IDF forces. (Source: @New_security8200/202129)

Israeli military intelligence indicates Hamas is regrouping for renewed war, producing hundreds of IEDs and anti-tank missiles monthly, recruiting fighters aged 18-22, conducting training for elite unit (Nukhba) members, attempting to smuggle drones and communications equipment from Sinai, and rehabilitating underground infrastructure throughout Gaza. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80249)
IDF Southern Command forces are deployed across the Gaza Strip in accordance with the agreement and will continue to act to remove any immediate threat. (Source: @New_security8200/202127)

Sgt. David Hazutt of the Golani Brigade was killed in a Hezbollah militant encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; the militant was later located in a nearby building and eliminated by IDF forces after extensive sweeps. (Source: @New_security8200/202132, @New_security8200/202136)

The IDF is preparing to demolish a large Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, with Upper Galilee residents warned to expect a high-intensity explosion in the coming hours. (Source: @manniefabian/58699)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir described the agreement with Lebanon's government as historic and significant, noting that IDF operational intensity and military achievements in recent months created the conditions for it; the test now is implementation by both sides. (Source: @idf_telegram/24464)

The Lebanon-Israel security framework has shifted from a fixed timeline to field-based assessment, with each implementation step contingent on practical success; a pilot project for Lebanese army entry into specific areas to disarm Hezbollah is set to begin within coming weeks. (Source: @SharghDaily/168636)

Beirut requested that Washington not publish a confidential security annex to the Lebanon-Israel agreement to preserve the government's room for internal management with Hezbollah. (Source: @SharghDaily/168636)
Lebanese MP Nauf Al-Mousawi stated that President Joseph Aoun asked the army commander to resign. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373451)
A blast heard in southern Lebanon was attributed to the IDF detonating infrastructure in Majdalzoun. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373447)

Iraqi security forces conducted a sweeping wave of arrests in Baghdad overnight, including sitting politicians and government figures; 47 officials have been arrested on corruption charges so far. (Source: @asafroz/50811, @asafroz/50814, @shin_persian/7266)

Large Iraqi security deployments, including tanks, were reported in Baghdad's Green Zone, which houses foreign embassies and government buildings, against the backdrop of the arrest wave targeting senior officials. (Source: @asafroz/50811)

Among those arrested on corruption charges were: head of Azm Alliance Mithaa al-Samarrai, and MPs Ziyad al-Janabi, Bahaa al-Nouri, Mohammed al-Karbouli, Alia Nasif, Mohammed Jamil al-Mayahi, and Hasan al-Khafaji. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80218)
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Iraq's new PM al-Zaidi in Baghdad as part of an official visit to Iraq. (Source: @presstv/196030)
Ashwaq al-Jabouri is in custody in Iraq. (Source: @shin_persian/7283)
A massive fire occurred at the Plaza hotel in Erbil, Iraq. (Source: @presstv/196033)
Syrians from Daraa continue to block roads to Israeli forces. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373433)
Iran reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait according to unconfirmed security reports from the field. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93285)
Iran reportedly fired missiles at Bahrain according to unconfirmed security reports from the field. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93283)

The US military launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz early Sunday following a Saturday morning attack on a commercial oil tanker in the waterway. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715)
Qatar's Ministry of Interior issued a statement regarding an incident involving a marine vessel. (Source: @shin_persian/7272)

Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely reduced, with only approximately 50 oil tankers and 40 containers transiting in June 2026 compared to 1,150 tankers and 400 containers in December 2025. (Source: @SharghDaily/168642)

key_developments: US military launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz early Sunday following a Saturday attack on a commercial oil tanker, marking direct US-Iran military escalation in the critical waterway. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715)

IDF Sgt. David Hazutt, 21, a Golani Brigade platoon commander, was killed in a Hezbollah militant encounter in Dir Siran, southern Lebanon; the militant was subsequently located and eliminated in a nearby building. (Source: @New_security8200/202132, @New_security8200/202136)

IDF airstrikes in Gaza eliminated multiple senior Hamas commanders: Nukhba squad commander Ziada in Shati, Khan Younis tunnel unit head Nagar, and Central Camps naval police commander Shihata with two deputies. (Source: @New_security8200/202127, @New_security8200/202129)

Explosions struck Iranian territory in Sirik (communications tower and naval base in Tahroviye), Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, and Bandar Lengeh areas, with projectiles hitting military infrastructure. (Source: @VahidOnline/76715, @VahidOnline/76721)

Iraqi security forces arrested 47 officials on corruption charges in a sweeping Baghdad operation that included sitting MPs and government figures, with tank deployments in the Green Zone. (Source: @asafroz/50811, @shin_persian/7266, @naya_foriraq/80218)

IDF Chief of Staff Zamir approved Northern Command plans, declaring the Lebanon agreement historic and stating Israel is prepared for rapid warfare in Lebanon and Iran if required. (Source: @idf_telegram/24464, @inon_yttach/9195)

Iran reportedly fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain according to unconfirmed field security reports, broadening the regional confrontation to Gulf states. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93285, @lelotsenzura/93283)
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level across the region is sharply elevated due to simultaneous escalatory dynamics: direct US-Iran military exchanges in the Strait of Hormuz represent the most dangerous flashpoint, with the risk of further Iranian retaliation against Gulf state targets (Kuwait, Bahrain) and continued disruption of maritime traffic. In Lebanon, Hezbollah's continued operational capability was demonstrated by the lethal engagement in Dir Siran that killed an IDF officer, even as the Lebanese framework agreement faces an uncertain implementation test. In Gaza, Hamas's ongoing reconstitution efforts — including mass production of IEDs and anti-tank missiles, elite unit training, and Sinai smuggling routes — present a persistent threat that Israeli intelligence assesses could lead to renewed large-scale conflict, despite US opposition to such an escalation.
sections.regional_response: The United States is actively engaged on multiple fronts: conducting direct military strikes against Iranian targets in the Strait of Hormuz while simultaneously opposing Israeli plans to return to full-scale combat in Gaza, preferring to maintain the status quo under existing agreements and advance President Trump's peace framework. Iraq is experiencing significant internal turbulence with a sweeping anti-corruption crackdown targeting senior political figures, even as it hosts diplomatic engagement between Iranian FM Araghchi and the new Iraqi PM. Lebanon's government is attempting to manage the delicate internal balance with Hezbollah by requesting confidentiality on security annexes while preparing a pilot disarmament project, even as domestic political tensions surface with the reported request for the army commander's resignation.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Israeli military intelligence assessment delivered to the US paints a concerning picture of Hamas's recovery trajectory: the organization is producing hundreds of IEDs and anti-tank missiles per month, recruiting a new cohort of 18-22 year old fighters, rehabilitating underground infrastructure across Gaza, and attempting to smuggle drones and communications equipment from Sinai — all indicators of systematic war preparation. The Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic data showing a collapse from 1,150 tankers in December 2025 to only 50 in June 2026 confirms the severe economic and logistical impact of the ongoing confrontation, with IRIB confirming that passage still requires IRGC coordination. The cancellation of the planned Switzerland talks between Tehran and Washington, combined with Iran's reported missile strikes against Kuwait and Bahrain, suggests the diplomatic track has deteriorated significantly in parallel with the military escalation.
